data.name: After downloading, you may want to learn how to
data.value: After downloading, you might like to learn how to

data.name: burn the ISO to a USB drive
data.value: transfer the ISO to a USB drive

data.name: Feeling generous? AnduinOS works based on donations from users. We are grateful for your support.
data.value: Feeling generous? AnduinOS relies on donations from users. We are grateful for your support.

data.name: If you are new to AnduinOS, you may also want to check out the
data.value: If you are new to AnduinOS, you might also like to have a look at the

data.name: Something not working? Have a suggestion? Post about it to discussion.
data.value: Something not working? Got a suggestion? Post about it on the discussion forum.

data.name: Take a look at documentation, you may find your answers there.
data.value: Have a look at the documentation; you may find your answers there.

data.name: After downloading the ISO file, you should verify its integrity to ensure it has not been tampered with.
data.value: After downloading the ISO file, you should verify its integrity to ensure it has not been tampered with.

data.name: On Linux or macOS, use the sha256sum command. On Windows, use 7-Zip.
data.value: On Linux or macOS, use the sha256sum command. On Windows, use 7-Zip.

data.name: Compare the output with the checksum provided on the release page (files ending with .sha256). If the checksums match, the ISO file is valid and untampered.
data.value: Compare the output with the checksum provided on the release page (files ending with .sha256). If the checksums match, the ISO file is valid and untampered.

data.name: Create a bootable USB drive to install AnduinOS on your computer.
data.value: Create a bootable USB drive to install AnduinOS on your computer.

data.name: Learn how to create a bootable USB drive
data.value: Learn how to create a bootable USB drive

data.name: Follow the steps below to get started with your new operating system.
data.value: Follow the steps below to get started with your new operating system.

data.name: . If the checksums match, the ISO file is valid and untampered.
data.value: . If the checksums match, the ISO file is valid and untampered.
